medications.indication_rationale: Apply to the entire sole and all web spaces daily for 2-4 weeks (moccasin type needs whole-foot treatment).
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to the whole foot daily for 2-4 weeks (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: miconazole 2% / hydrocortisone 1% combination cream (brief) to the fissure
medications.indication_rationale: Brief course (~3 days) to settle the acutely inflamed painful fissure while treating fungus.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to the fissure twice daily for ~3 days (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: prolonged topical steroid to the feet
medications.indication_rationale: Prolonged steroid masks/worsens tinea — limit to a few days on the fissure only.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Dry carefully between the toes after every shower (tissue/cool hairdryer), wear moisture-wicking/cotton socks changed mid-shift, air the feet, and treat boots with antifungal powder.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the moisture the fungus needs and prevents fissuring/recurrence.

patient_education_points.point: This is athlete's foot with a deep split between the toes. The antifungal cream treats the fungus (use it on the whole foot for weeks), and a brief combination cream settles the painful crack — but keeping the feet bone-dry is the most important part.
Dry between the toes, change socks mid-shift, air your feet, and powder your boots. Don't force the toes apart.
Because the split is an open door for infection, go to the emergency department if the top of your foot becomes red/hot/swollen, red streaks travel up your leg, or you develop a fever/shivering.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Fissure closure and absence of cellulitis; ongoing antifungal for the moccasin scaling.

must_include_items: Diagnose tinea pedis with fissuring and screen for ascending cellulitis
Whole-foot antifungal + brief combination for the fissure + strict drying/footwear
Treat concurrent tinea cruris/nails if present
acceptable_alternatives: Oral antifungal for extensive/refractory disease or nail involvement
errors_of_omission: Not treating the moccasin scaling (whole foot)
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires whole-foot antifungal + strict drying + a strong cellulitis safety-net given the open fissure.
